content: ActionBar is a horizontal toolbar component from @gravity-ui/navigation with grouped action items. Each group can contain buttons, selects, or custom content. Supports responsive collapsing of items into a dropdown menu. Import: import {ActionBar} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

ActionBar props: className (string) additional CSS class. ActionBar.Group subcomponent: items (ActionBarItem[]) items in the group; pull ('left' | 'right') alignment direction. ActionBarItem types include button, select, and custom renderers.
ActionBar usage with left and right aligned groups.
code_examples:
<ActionBar>
  <ActionBar.Group pull="left">
    <ActionBar.Item>
      <Button view="flat" onClick={handleBack}>Back</Button>
    </ActionBar.Item>
  </ActionBar.Group>
  <ActionBar.Group pull="right">
    <ActionBar.Item>
      <Button view="action" onClick={handleSave}>Save</Button>
    </ActionBar.Item>
  </ActionBar.Group>
</ActionBar>

content: AsideHeader is a collapsible sidebar navigation component from @gravity-ui/navigation. It renders a logo, menu items with icons and nested items, a panel toggle, and a footer section. Supports compact and expanded modes with smooth animations. Import: import {AsideHeader} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

AsideHeader props: menuItems (MenuItem[], required) navigation menu items; logo (LogoProps, required) logo configuration; className (string) additional CSS class; compact (boolean) controlled compact mode; defaultCompact (boolean) uncontrolled initial compact state; headerDecoration (boolean) enables header decoration; hideCollapseButton (boolean) hides the collapse toggle; onChangeCompact (function) compact state change callback; panelItems (PanelItem[]) additional panel items; renderContent (function) renders main content area; renderFooter (function) renders footer section; topAlert (TopAlertProps) top alert configuration.
AsideHeader usage with menu items and logo.
code_examples:
<AsideHeader
  logo={{text: 'My App', href: '/'}}
  menuItems={[
    {id: 'home', title: 'Home', icon: HomeIcon, link: '/'},
    {id: 'settings', title: 'Settings', icon: GearIcon, link: '/settings'},
  ]}
  renderContent={() => <MainContent />}
/>

content: Footer is a page footer component from @gravity-ui/navigation with logo, navigation links organized in columns, social links, and copyright text. Supports light and dark color themes. Import: import {Footer} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

Footer props: className (string) additional CSS class; copyright (string) copyright text; logo (FooterLogoProps) logo configuration; links (FooterColumn[]) navigation link columns; socialLinks (FooterSocialLink[]) social network links; theme ('light' | 'dark') color theme.
Footer usage with logo, links, and copyright.
code_examples:
<Footer
  logo={{src: '/logo.svg', alt: 'My App', href: '/'}}
  copyright="© 2024 My Company"
  links={[
    {
      title: 'Product',
      links: [
        {text: 'Features', url: '/features'},
        {text: 'Pricing', url: '/pricing'},
      ],
    },
  ]}
  socialLinks={[
    {icon: GithubIcon, url: 'https://github.com/example'},
  ]}
/>

content: HotkeysPanel is a component from @gravity-ui/navigation that renders a side panel listing available keyboard shortcuts grouped by category. Displays each shortcut with its key combination and description. Import: import {HotkeysPanel} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

HotkeysPanel props: groups (HotkeyGroup[], required) array of hotkey groups; onClose (function, required) close handler; visible (boolean, required) controls panel visibility; className (string) additional CSS class; title (string) panel title.
HotkeysPanel usage with grouped keyboard shortcuts.
code_examples:
<HotkeysPanel
  visible={showHotkeys}
  onClose={() => setShowHotkeys(false)}
  title="Keyboard shortcuts"
  groups={[
    {
      title: 'Navigation',
      items: [
        {keys: ['g', 'h'], description: 'Go to home'},
        {keys: ['g', 's'], description: 'Go to settings'},
      ],
    },
  ]}
/>

content: Logo is a component from @gravity-ui/navigation that renders a product or company logo with optional text label. Used as the header element in AsideHeader and MobileHeader navigation components. Import: import {Logo} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

Logo props: icon (React.FC, required) logo icon component; className (string) additional CSS class; compact (boolean) compact display mode (icon only); href (string) logo link URL; iconSize (number) icon size in pixels; onClick (function) click handler; text (string) logo text label.
Logo usage with icon and text.

MobileHeader is a mobile navigation component from @gravity-ui/navigation with a hamburger menu that opens a full-screen drawer. Supports logo, navigation items, and custom content areas for sidepanel. Import: import {MobileHeader} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

MobileHeader props: logo (LogoProps, required) logo configuration; burgerMenu (MobileMenuItem[]) hamburger menu items; className (string) additional CSS class; contentClassName (string) content area CSS class; onClosePanel (function) panel close callback; onOpenPanel (function) panel open callback; renderContent (function) renders main content; renderFooter (function) renders footer in drawer; sideItemRenderContent (function) renders side panel content.
MobileHeader usage for a mobile app layout.
code_examples:
<MobileHeader
  logo={{icon: AppIcon, text: 'My App', href: '/'}}
  burgerMenu={[
    {id: 'home', title: 'Home', icon: HomeIcon, link: '/'},
    {id: 'profile', title: 'Profile', icon: UserIcon, link: '/profile'},
  ]}
  renderContent={() => <MainContent />}
/>

content: Settings is a panel component from @gravity-ui/navigation that renders settings with sections and individual setting items. Supports search filtering, custom renderers for setting values, and loading state. Import: import {Settings} from '@gravity-ui/navigation';

Settings props: className (string) additional CSS class; initialPage (string) initially active settings page id; loading (boolean) shows loading state; onPageChange (function) callback on page navigation; renderRightAdornment (function) renders right side adornment. Settings.Section subcomponent: header (string) section title; items (SettingItem[]) settings items. SettingItem has title, renderValue, and optional description.
Settings usage with sections and items.
code_examples:
<Settings initialPage="appearance">
  <Settings.Page id="appearance" title="Appearance" icon={PaletteIcon}>
    <Settings.Section header="Theme">
      <Settings.Item title="Color theme" renderValue={() => <Select options={themes} />} />
      <Settings.Item title="Font size" renderValue={() => <Select options={fontSizes} />} />
    </Settings.Section>
  </Settings.Page>
</Settings>
